# Security Policy

## Reporting a vulnerability

Please report security issues **privately** — do not open a public issue.

- Preferred: use GitHub's [private vulnerability reporting](https://github.com/Ceaustin117/ai-egg-index/security/advisories/new) ("Report a vulnerability" on the Security tab).
- Or email: ceaustin117@gmail.com

We aim to acknowledge reports within **7 days**.

## Scope / threat model

The AI Egg Index is an evaluation harness, not a hosted service, so the relevant
threat surface is different from a typical web app:

- **Untrusted model output** — the harness executes/processes text generated by
  external LLMs (including, for some benchmarks, generated code). Issues that could
  let model output escape its sandbox or execute unintended code are in scope.
- **Result / benchmark tampering** — anything that lets a contribution silently
  alter scores, bypass the judge, or game the leaderboard.
- **Dataset poisoning** — malicious prompt/rubric changes designed to corrupt scoring.
- **Supply chain** — vulnerable or malicious dependencies in `requirements.txt`.

Out of scope: rate limits / quotas on third-party free-tier APIs (expected and
handled gracefully), and the cosmetic rendering of the public leaderboard.

## Supported versions

This is an actively developed project; only the latest `main` and most recent
release are supported. Older tagged methodology versions are kept for historical
comparability but are not patched.
